intro: Lepidonia lankesteri, also known as Lepidonia, is a tropical plant species native to Central America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. Lepidonia has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions including wounds and infections. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lepidonia is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional medicine remedies, such as topical creams and ointments, to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
